globalRule.description: Flags records where a Subscriber_ID has a positive Monthly_Charge but is missing Activation_Date, Plan_ID, or SIM_Type, crucial for high-impact churn models.
globalRule.expression: SELECT * FROM INTERNALQUERY WHERE @Subscriber_ID@ IS NOT NULL AND @Monthly_Charge@ > 0 AND (@Activation_Date@ IS NULL OR @Plan_ID@ IS NULL OR @SIM_Type@ IS NULL);

globalRule.description: Flags records where a Plan_ID has a zero or null Monthly_Charge for a Subscriber_ID within a specific Circle, indicating a potential billing error.
globalRule.expression: SELECT t1.* FROM INTERNALQUERY t1 JOIN ( SELECT @Plan_ID@, @Circle@ FROM INTERNALQUERY WHERE @Monthly_Charge@ <= 0 OR @Monthly_Charge@ IS NULL GROUP BY @Plan_ID@, @Circle@ HAVING COUNT(*) > 0 ) t2 ON t1.@Plan_ID@ = t2.@Plan_ID@ AND t1.@Circle@ = t2.@Circle@ WHERE t1.@Subscriber_ID@ IS NOT NULL;

globalRule.description: Identifies records where a Plan_ID has a Monthly_Charge inconsistent with its Activation_Date, especially for accounts with a non-null Account_Status.
globalRule.expression: SELECT t1.* FROM INTERNALQUERY t1 JOIN ( SELECT @Plan_ID@, AVG(@Monthly_Charge@) as plan_avg_charge FROM INTERNALQUERY WHERE @Monthly_Charge@ IS NOT NULL GROUP BY @Plan_ID@ ) t2 ON t1.@Plan_ID@ = t2.@Plan_ID@ WHERE t1.@Monthly_Charge@ IS NOT NULL AND t1.@Account_Status@ IS NOT NULL AND (t1.@Monthly_Charge@ > (t2.plan_avg_charge * 2) OR t1.@Monthly_Charge@ < (t2.plan_avg_charge * 0.5)) AND @Activation_Date@ < CURRENT_DATE();

globalRule.description: Flags Plan_ID records where the Monthly_Charge deviates significantly from the average for that SIM_Type and Plan_Name.
globalRule.expression: SELECT t1.* FROM INTERNALQUERY t1 JOIN ( SELECT @Plan_ID@, @SIM_Type@, @Plan_Name@, AVG(@Monthly_Charge@) as avg_charge FROM INTERNALQUERY WHERE @Monthly_Charge@ IS NOT NULL GROUP BY @Plan_ID@, @SIM_Type@, @Plan_Name@ ) t2 ON t1.@Plan_ID@ = t2.@Plan_ID@ AND t1.@SIM_Type@ = t2.@SIM_Type@ AND t1.@Plan_Name@ = t2.@Plan_Name@ WHERE t1.@Monthly_Charge@ > (t2.avg_charge * 1.5) OR t1.@Monthly_Charge@ < (t2.avg_charge * 0.5);

globalRule.description: Identifies records where a Mobile_Number is associated with more than one unique Subscriber_ID, indicating data inconsistency for Customer_Name.
globalRule.expression: SELECT t1.* FROM INTERNALQUERY t1 JOIN ( SELECT @Mobile_Number@, COUNT(DISTINCT @Subscriber_ID@) as subscriber_count FROM INTERNALQUERY GROUP BY @Mobile_Number@ HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT @Subscriber_ID@) > 1 ) t2 ON t1.@Mobile_Number@ = t2.@Mobile_Number@ WHERE t1.@Customer_Name@ IS NOT NULL;
